Summary: Create referral/ambassador test plan
Description:
HIERARCHY / LINKAGE:
Issue TGTM-125 is a Story under Epic TGTM-11 - Run pre-launch, launch, post-launch, developer, investor, and institutional campaigns. Direct child issues: TGTM-991 - Define the post-test reporting and analysis plan; TGTM-990 - Create the experiment QA and launch checklist; TGTM-989 - Draft referral and ambassador messaging for the test; TGTM-988 - Define the referral journey and campaign workflow; TGTM-987 - Design the referral offer structure, incentives, and reward logic; TGTM-986 - Prepare referral tracking and attribution requirements; TGTM-985 - Document the referral and ambassador test audience, eligibility, and segmentation rules; TGTM-984 - Define month 6 referral and ambassador test objectives, hypotheses, and success metrics.

DOMAIN:
Referral / Ambassador Growth Test

ORIGINAL INTENT:
Prepare month 6 scale test for investor referrals:

DOMAIN: Referral / Ambassador Growth Test

OBJECTIVE: Design a controlled Month 6 referral and ambassador test that turns early believers, investors, developer partners, brokers, advisors, and ecosystem connectors into a measurable acquisition channel without creating uncontrolled compliance, brand, or investor-suitability risk.

TARGET AUDIENCES: Existing investor waitlist members, early platform users, private beta participants, developer-side champions, real estate advisors, family-office connectors, Dubai ecosystem operators, fintech/proptech community members, and professional-service partners who can introduce qualified investors or developers.

SCOPE OF WORK: Define the referral hypothesis, referral audiences, eligibility rules, campaign mechanics, incentive options, tracking model, approval workflow, referral qualification criteria, communication templates, and reporting cadence. Separate B2C investor referrals from developer/institutional introductions because each audience has a different sales cycle, compliance sensitivity, qualification standard, and conversion path.

KEY DELIVERABLES: Referral program brief; ambassador persona definitions; referral source taxonomy; invitation copy; landing-page copy; tracking spreadsheet or CRM tagging model; referral qualification checklist; partner outreach scripts; fraud/spam prevention rules; incentive recommendation; campaign reporting dashboard; post-test readout template.

CHANNELS / TACTICS: Founder-led introductions, LinkedIn DM referrals, WhatsApp/private community sharing, investor webinar referrals, developer-network introductions, advisor referral packs, closed investor briefings, and selective ambassador invitations to people who already understand the Blocks Estate thesis.

MESSAGE ANGLES: "Invite qualified investors into curated Dubai real estate access"; "Introduce developers to a new capital formation channel"; "Help trusted contacts understand digital real estate investment products before the market becomes crowded"; "Private access first, public scale later."

OPERATING CADENCE: Week 1: finalize test design and eligibility. Week 2: prepare copy, tracking, and compliance guardrails. Weeks 3-6: launch controlled test with selected referrers. Weeks 7-8: review conversion quality, CAC proxy, referral-source quality, and operational friction before deciding whether to scale.

KPIs: Number of approved ambassadors; number of referral invitations sent; referral-to-landing-page conversion; qualified investor referrals; qualified developer introductions; meeting-booking rate; waitlist conversion rate; cost per qualified referral; percentage of referrals rejected for poor fit; top 10 referrers by qualified contribution.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Referral test brief is approved; at least 3 referrer personas are defined; all referral links or tracking tags are ready; investor and developer referral paths are separated; invitation and follow-up copy is approved; minimum qualification criteria are documented; campaign dashboard is created; referral-source reporting is available by segment; test readout template includes learnings, recommendation, and scale/no-scale decision.

DEPENDENCIES: CRM or spreadsheet tracking; approved investor/developer qualification criteria; brand messaging pack; legal/compliance review of incentive language; landing page or waitlist flow; founder approval on ambassador list.

RISKS / CONTROLS: Avoid implying guaranteed returns, asset availability, regulatory approval, or investment advice. Keep incentives simple and compliant. Do not allow public spam-style sharing before the brand has trust signals. Track referral quality, not only volume.

DONE MEANS: Blocks Estate can run a small, measurable referral/ambassador pilot and decide whether referrals should become a repeatable GTM channel.

OBJECTIVE:
Design a Month 6 controlled referral and ambassador test that turns early believers, investors, partners, and advisors into measurable acquisition channels.

HOW WE ARE DOING THIS:
Treat this Story as a complete campaign workstream: define audience, message, offer, CTA, channel mix, assets, approvals, tracking, launch process, and learning loop. The child Tasks should collectively produce the campaign brief, copy, creative, landing or outreach flow, review approvals, QA checklist, publishing plan, and measurement/reporting package.

PRELIMINARY SOLUTION:
Create a referral test operating model with objectives/hypotheses, eligibility rules, tracking and attribution requirements, incentive logic, workflow map, ambassador messaging, QA checklist, and post-test scale recommendation.

AUDIENCE / CHANNELS / KPIS:
Audience: existing waitlist members, early investors, ambassadors, advisor networks, broker/developer introducers, partner contacts, and trusted founder/community relationships. Channels: unique referral links, ambassador outreach, email/WhatsApp/DM invitations, partner introductions, CRM tasks, referral landing/forms, and performance dashboard. KPIs: referrals submitted, qualified referred leads, referral conversion rate, ambassador activation rate, cost per qualified referral, reward eligibility, and fraud/duplicate rate.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
Use a single campaign workspace for all assets and decisions; keep source-of-truth copy separate from design exports; apply legal/compliance review before publication; ensure all CTAs route to CRM or a tracked destination; document first 24-hour monitoring responsibilities.

DEPENDENCIES / INPUTS:
Parent Epic strategy, positioning and messaging guide, target audience definition, approved claims, design/brand templates, channel access, CRM and analytics configuration, and campaign owner sign-off.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
campaign brief is complete and tied to the parent epic objective; required child tasks are defined, sequenced, and ready for execution; solution, channel plan, success metrics, approvals, and launch-readiness criteria are documented.

MESSAGE ANGLES: "Dubai real estate access is changing"; "From traditional property buying to digital real estate participation"; "Curated access, clearer information, lower entry barriers"; "Trust and transparency before scale"; "A platform built between developers and investors."

OPERATING CADENCE: Month 1: define story, waitlist, content pillars, and tracking. Month 2: publish education content, run founder outreach, and gather market objections. Month 3: deepen waitlist nurture, segment leads, and prepare launch audience for first access.

KPIs: Waitlist signups; qualified leads; landing-page conversion; content engagement; founder profile engagement; email subscriber growth; lead-source quality; investor/developer questions collected; number of warm intro opportunities; pre-launch survey responses; launch-ready audience size.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Messaging house is approved; waitlist page is live or ready; at least 4 content pillars are defined; 30/60/90-day content calendar is created; lead capture and CRM tagging are working; FAQ covers core investor and developer questions; analytics are installed; founder outreach scripts are ready; weekly reporting cadence is defined.

DEPENDENCIES: Brand positioning; website or landing page; CRM; analytics; founder narrative; initial developer/investor assumptions; compliance-safe claims; design assets.

RISKS / CONTROLS: Avoid launching sales claims before trust is established. Do not imply finalized assets, guaranteed returns, or regulatory outcomes. Use pre-launch as a learning engine: collect objections, questions, audience quality, and message resonance.

DONE MEANS: Blocks Estate has a credible pre-launch demand engine that builds the right audience, captures qualified interest, and prepares the market for the first Dubai launch.

OBJECTIVE:
Build trust, awareness, and an early-access audience before selling by explaining why Dubai real estate access is changing and why Blocks Estate is credible.

HOW WE ARE DOING THIS:
Treat this Story as a complete campaign workstream: define audience, message, offer, CTA, channel mix, assets, approvals, tracking, launch process, and learning loop. The child Tasks should collectively produce the campaign brief, copy, creative, landing or outreach flow, review approvals, QA checklist, publishing plan, and measurement/reporting package.

PRELIMINARY SOLUTION:
Create a bilingual pre-launch trust campaign with a clear market-change narrative, proof-point library, waitlist CTA, modular English/Arabic copy pack, hero visual system, legal/commercial review path, and QA checklist before publication.

AUDIENCE / CHANNELS / KPIS:
Audience: early investors, real-estate-interested professionals, high-intent prospects, founders network, ecosystem partners, and bilingual UAE/GCC audiences. Channels: website/landing page, LinkedIn, email, founder posts, partner shares, WhatsApp/DM follow-up, and Arabic localized placements. KPIs: waitlist sign-ups, qualified early-access leads, landing-page conversion rate, content engagement, audience growth, email/DM replies, and trust-content completion.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
Use a single campaign workspace for all assets and decisions; keep source-of-truth copy separate from design exports; apply legal/compliance review before publication; ensure all CTAs route to CRM or a tracked destination; document first 24-hour monitoring responsibilities.

DEPENDENCIES / INPUTS:
Parent Epic strategy, positioning and messaging guide, target audience definition, approved claims, design/brand templates, channel access, CRM and analytics configuration, and campaign owner sign-off.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
campaign brief is complete and tied to the parent epic objective; required child tasks are defined, sequenced, and ready for execution; solution, channel plan, success metrics, approvals, and launch-readiness criteria are documented.

Summary: Run pre-launch, launch, post-launch, developer, investor, and institutional campaigns
Description:
HIERARCHY / LINKAGE:
Issue TGTM-11 is the parent Epic for the Campaigns domain. Direct child Stories: TGTM-125 - Create referral/ambassador test plan; TGTM-122 - Build institutional campaign: Infrastructure for Responsible Real Estate Digital Investment Products; TGTM-121 - Build investor campaign: Access Curated Dubai Real Estate Opportunities with Lower Entry Tickets; TGTM-120 - Build developer campaign: Unlock a New Capital Channel for Dubai Real Estate; TGTM-119 - Build post-launch campaign: From Asset to Digital Offering; TGTM-118 - Build launch campaign: Curated Dubai Real Estate, Digitally Accessible; TGTM-117 - Build pre-launch campaign: Dubai Real Estate Access Is Changing.

ORIGINAL INTENT:
Operationalize the campaign strategy across waitlist/private access, developer assessments, first asset launch, case studies, investor education, and institutional partner briefings.

DOMAIN: Campaign System Epic - Pre-Launch, Launch, Post-Launch, Developer, Investor, Institutional, and Referral Campaigns

OBJECTIVE: Operationalize the full Blocks Estate GTM campaign system for the Dubai-first launch and regional expansion path. This epic covers the campaign architecture needed to build trust before launch, acquire qualified investors, secure developer supply, create institutional credibility, execute the first launch, convert launch activity into proof, and test referrals as a scale channel.

STRATEGIC CONTEXT: Blocks Estate must communicate to multiple audiences at once without losing focus. Developers need a new capital/distribution channel. Investors need curated access, education, and trust. Institutional partners need governance, compliance discipline, and responsible infrastructure. The market needs proof that Blocks Estate is not hype, not a generic listing site, and not an unstructured tokenization experiment.

INCLUDED DOMAINS: Pre-launch awareness and education; first launch activation; post-launch proof and case studies; developer acquisition; investor acquisition and waitlist nurturing; institutional partner/family-office campaign; referral and ambassador scale test.

EPIC SCOPE: Define messaging, audience segmentation, channel strategy, deliverables, campaign assets, CRM tracking, lead qualification, KPIs, acceptance criteria, dependencies, risk controls, reporting cadence, and handoffs between campaigns. Ensure all campaigns share one strategic narrative while giving each audience its own problem, value proposition, proof points, and conversion path.

CORE DELIVERABLES: Master GTM campaign map; audience segmentation model; messaging architecture; content calendar; campaign-specific briefs; landing/waitlist strategy; CRM tagging and reporting model; developer target list; investor nurture flow; institutional partner target list; launch checklist; post-launch proof system; referral test plan; objection-handling library; founder-led outreach scripts; campaign dashboard.

OPERATING MODEL: Pre-launch builds trust and captures demand. Developer campaign builds supply. Investor campaign qualifies demand. Institutional campaign builds credibility and partner leverage. Launch campaign converts readiness into action. Post-launch campaign creates proof. Referral campaign tests scalable growth from early believers.

GOVERNANCE: All campaign claims must be reviewed for investment-risk sensitivity, regulatory caution, brand consistency, and audience fit. Messaging should emphasize transparency, curation, education, responsible access, and quality control. The campaign system must avoid guaranteed-return claims, liquidity promises, regulatory overstatements, or inflated market hype.

KPIs: Qualified investor leads, developer meetings, institutional conversations, waitlist conversion, launch activation, content engagement, partner introductions, cost per qualified lead if paid media is used, campaign-to-meeting conversion, developer feasibility submissions, launch conversion, proof-asset engagement, referral quality, and objection reduction over time.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: All child campaign stories have approved briefs; each campaign has clear audience, message, channel, CTA, deliverables, KPI, dependency, and risk-control sections; campaign reporting dashboard exists; CRM tags support attribution; content calendar is aligned with launch timeline; founder outreach scripts are ready; developer/investor/institutional objections are documented; launch and post-launch handoffs are defined.

DEPENDENCIES: Product narrative; business plan; legal/compliance review; landing page; CRM; analytics; founder availability; content/design capacity; developer pipeline; first asset/developer selection; investor qualification logic; institutional diligence pack.

DONE MEANS: Blocks Estate has a detailed, import-ready GTM campaign workstream that can guide execution across marketing, sales, founder outreach, partnerships, launch, and post-launch proof generation.

HOW WE ARE DOING THIS:
Run the GTM campaign system as one coordinated operating program instead of isolated marketing tasks. The work is broken into pre-launch trust building, first launch conversion, post-launch proof, developer acquisition, investor demand generation, institutional credibility, and referral/ambassador testing. Each Story should use the same operating pattern: define audience and offer, prepare campaign assets, validate copy and claims, publish through approved channels, track source-to-conversion performance, and capture learnings for the next campaign.

PRELIMINARY SOLUTION:
Create a campaign control-tower model for Blocks Estate with one master campaign tracker, one UTM/source naming convention, one approval workflow, one asset repository, one CRM tagging approach, and one post-campaign learning template. Use each child Story as a campaign pod with its own brief, assets, channel plan, QA checklist, and launch/reporting owner.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
Sequence the work so trust and waitlist infrastructure are ready before launch, launch assets are approved before paid/public distribution, post-launch proof is captured immediately after the first asset release, developer outreach is CRM-driven, investor education is segmented, institutional materials are credibility-first, and the referral test is only launched once tracking and reward logic are validated.

DEPENDENCIES / INPUTS:
GTM strategy, positioning and messaging system, legal/compliance claim rules, product readiness, CRM setup, analytics setup, design templates, landing-page capability, founder availability, developer/investor/institutional lists, and campaign owners.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
all seven campaign tracks have a clear strategy, owner, deliverables, approval path, launch plan, measurement plan, and learning loop; cross-campaign dependencies, reusable assets, and channel conflicts are documented; campaign execution can be monitored through one operating rhythm and one campaign status view.
